summary: SurrealDB POC migration. Initial 3-function POC succeeded, full port of relations layer + cleanup + audit completed across 14 commits on poc/surrealdb-relations branch. Then completed A1 (full-audit sections) and A2 (review classify-relations). A3 (build_active_fact_index) pending — paused due to context budget.

completed_tasks.name: Full relations.py port (missing_support, cleanup, remaining public API)
Wire cmd_audit / cmd_review / cmd_plan to SurrealDB
Delete old Python + rename _surreal suffix off + final docs

remaining_tasks.name: Port build_active_fact_index from fact_index.py to SurrealDB

deferred_decisions.rationale: Expert panel (round 3) + user agreed: current Python (3-rule structure with same-name + alias-exact + SequenceMatcher) is well-tuned for the substance-deduplication domain. SurrealDB FTS-based or jaro-based alternatives have worse domain fit for this use case. Kaizen Master's dissent prevailed.

deferred_decisions.decision: DO NOT do Tier B (DEFINE TABLE SCHEMAFULL) yet
deferred_decisions.rationale: Requires philosophical decision: YAML or SurrealDB as primary source of truth. Big architectural shift. Logged for future discussion.

next_action: Implement A3: port planner.cards.fact_index.build_active_fact_index to SurrealDB. Single function port, ~60 LOC, low complexity. Add to planner/cards/relations_surreal.py (or new module if preferred). Replace call in planner/engine/plan.py:_build_schedule_output where build_active_fact_index is invoked. Likely fact_index.py becomes empty and can be deleted; verify all callers.
context_notes: Paused due to context budget (~66% utilization warning fired after A2 commit). User explicitly chose option 2 of 3 (push commits, resume next session). A3 fits within remaining budget but user prefers clean restart. All commits pushed to origin/poc/surrealdb-relations.

anti_patterns_discovered.description: When moving helpers between modules (specifically: moving stacks_for_surreal / pillbox_stack_names / dashboards_for_surreal from audit.py into relations_surreal.py during A2), maybe_patch_root's _MODULES list was not auto-updated. Tests using data_root=tmp_path silently broke because module-bound STACKS_PATH/DATA_DIR/DASHBOARDS_DIR in the new module wasn't being patched. Cost: one failed test cycle.
anti_patterns_discovered.mitigation: When introducing a new module that imports path constants from planner.io, add it to planner/engine/_root_patch.py:_MODULES. Already done for relations_surreal and audit_surreal.

anti_patterns_discovered.description: First two rounds of run_expert_panel on similar-substance metric choice implicitly framed Python SequenceMatcher as 'the thing being replaced' rather than a peer candidate. User had to explicitly correct this. Result: third round revisited with Python as legitimate option, Kaizen's 'don't port' position then prevailed.
anti_patterns_discovered.mitigation: When panel-discussing replacements, explicitly include the status quo as a labeled option with the same evaluation rigor as alternatives. Don't assume the user wants a change just because they invoked a 'replacement' frame.
